Islam Sayed
Posted on
Mar 24, 2019
How to read others' code?
#
webdev
#
discuss
I am wondering what are the best strategies to read others' written code, especially when it is not well commented, or documented. Add to that if it uses libraries and technologies that are new to you.
I heard about what is called "
reverse engineering
" and that it could help tracking the code and spotting bugs. What you know about it and how could one improve his/her ability to make the best out of it?

If I'm in a lang with decent refactor tools and I find variables or methods with names that don't seem to line up with that the codes does I like to rename them in my local branch. It just takes out an additional layer of trying to mentally map between the names and the intentions of the code
Another thing would be to try and write some unit tests (if they aren't already there). When I don't understand what it is trying to do then write them without any asserts even, at that point it is a segregated sandbox for observing behavior

Reverse engineering usually refers to code not handed to us. When you have the code in your hands there is not any special strategy most of the times or it might be dictated by what we want to accomplish with it. Usually you can get a general sense of structure and what it accomplishes and than focus and understand the parts that are relevant to your task. Lots of times running it with a debugger and stoping at certain points to inspect variables helps in making sense faster.
When it uses libraries that are new to me I try to understand the purpose they serve and see how they are used within the code and read more about them from documentation or other resources and usually go as deep as to make me understand in order to complete my task.  We can not really know the inner workings of ALL the libraries we use throughout our code.

We can not really know the inner workings of ALL the libraries we use throughout our code.

Generally it tends to involve muffled screaming.. but for me it comes down to a couple of key approaches.
1) breakpoints.  So many breakpoints.
2) comment the code yourself as you work through it.. if you can't work something out, leave a to-do and come back to it, but comment every live if you need to.  Writing it down generally helps me get a grip in my mind of it.
Actually, my first go at it generally involves fitting it up on a closed test server and turning on a SQL trace and taking a look at the SQL that is hitting the database.  From there I can generally understand a bit easier.  Obvious exceptions of course

How to Understand Coding Syntax and Logic
By
Nicholas Idoko
June 2, 2023
How to Understand Coding Syntax and Logic
Understanding the syntax and logic of coding is crucial for anyone who wants to become a skilled programmer. Coding syntax is the set of rules that dictate how to write proper programming language statements while coding logic determines how the program behaves and interacts with the user.
By grasping these concepts, programmers can create error-free, efficient, and robust code that meets the criteria of the application they are developing. In essence, coding syntax and logic are the backbone of any software development project, so it’s essential to develop a good understanding from the outset.
Having said that, coding is not an easy task, but by focusing on the fundamentals like syntax and logic, aspiring programmers can enhance their skills and tackle complex programming tasks more efficiently. Basically, knowing coding syntax and logic is the foundation upon which applications are built, and it is therefore crucial for any programmer looking to hone their skills.
Read:
What is the Time Investment to Learn Coding?
Understanding Coding Syntax
Coding is the process of writing instructions or commands that a computer can understand and execute. To write efficient and effective computer programs, it is crucial to master coding syntax and logic. In this article, we will take a closer look at coding syntax, its definition, and examples.

To write efficient and effective computer programs, it is crucial to master coding syntax and logic. In this article, we will take a closer look at coding syntax, its definition, and examples. We will also discuss why mastering coding syntax is essential for any aspiring programmer.
Definition of Coding Syntax
Coding syntax refers to the rules and guidelines that govern the structure and usage of programming language. It is a set of instructions or rules that must be followed when writing computer programs to ensure that they are accurate and functional. These rules are crucial in programming because they define the correct arrangement of commands, keywords, and symbols, which determine how the computer program will be executed.
For instance, if a programmer writes a program in a language such as Python, he or she must follow specific syntax rules to create a correct program. A single wrong character or missing keyword can result in a syntax error, which means that the computer cannot execute the program.
Read:
How to Start Coding: A Step-by-Step Guide
Examples of Coding Syntax
Here are some examples of coding syntax:
Using brackets to identify the start and end of a code block: { }
Ending a line with a semicolon: ;
Declaring a variable: var
Using parentheses to call a function: ()
Using quotes to define strings: ” “
These rules may vary depending on the programming language. For instance, in some programming languages like Python, indentation is critical to identify the start and end of a code block, while in others, such as C, curly brackets are used.
Read:
Can You Learn Coding with Online Courses?
Importance of Mastering Coding Syntax
Mastering coding syntax is critical for anyone who wants to become a proficient programmer. Here are some reasons why:
Accuracy:
Following the right coding syntax ensures that your program is free of syntax errors and will be executed correctly.
Readability:
Writing code that adheres to standard coding syntax makes it easier for other programmers to read and understand your code.
Consistency:
Using consistent coding syntax throughout your program makes it easier to maintain and update your code over time.
Efficiency:
Knowing coding syntax and following best coding practices can help you write code that executes faster and uses fewer system resources.
Furthermore, mastering coding syntax is essential because it lays the foundation for learning more advanced programming concepts.

Efficiency:
Knowing coding syntax and following best coding practices can help you write code that executes faster and uses fewer system resources.
Furthermore, mastering coding syntax is essential because it lays the foundation for learning more advanced programming concepts. By developing a deep understanding of coding syntax, you can move on to more complex programming concepts such as object-oriented programming (OOP) and data structures.
Coding syntax is the fundamental building block for any computer program. It is essential to master coding syntax to write efficient, reliable, and readable code. Following standard coding syntax rules ensures that your code is accurate, consistent, and efficient. So, take the time to learn coding syntax and apply it to your programming projects. It will pay off in the long run.
Read:
How to Choose a Coding Project for Practice
Understanding Coding Logic
Coding logic refers to the way a programmer thinks about solving a problem using code.
Examples of Coding Logic
Breaking down a problem into smaller parts or steps
Identifying patterns and similarities to create reusable code
Using conditional statements to control program flow
Looping through data to perform repetitive tasks
Debugging code by identifying and fixing bugs
Read:
What are Essential Coding Tools for Beginners?
Importance of Mastering Coding Logic
Without a strong understanding of coding logic, it can be difficult to create effective and efficient code.
Reduces errors:
Accurate coding logic helps programmers avoid bugs and other errors in their code.
Increases efficiency:
Effective coding logic allows developers to write code that runs quickly and smoothly.
Boosts creativity:
Mastering coding logic can help programmers find innovative solutions to complex problems.
Fosters collaboration:
A shared understanding of coding logic helps team members work together more effectively.
Enables flexibility:
Strong coding logic skills allow developers to adapt to changing project requirements and programming languages.
As you hone your coding skills, it’s important to make an effort to improve your coding logic skills as well. Consider working on coding exercises that emphasize logical thinking and problem-solving.
Additionally, when working on projects, take time to carefully plan out your code and think about the different ways you could approach a problem before writing any actual code.
By focusing on coding logic, you’ll not only produce better code, but you’ll also become a more skilled and versatile programmer overall.

By focusing on coding logic, you’ll not only produce better code, but you’ll also become a more skilled and versatile programmer overall.
Read:
How to Stay Motivated While Learning to Code
Tips for Understanding Coding Syntax and Logic
When it comes to understanding coding syntax and logic, there are a few tips that can help make the learning process easier. Below are four strategies that you can use to improve your understanding and make coding a more enjoyable experience.
Regular Practice
Practice is crucial to understanding coding syntax and logic. You cannot become proficient in coding if you don’t practice frequently.
Allocate time each day or week to practice coding, even if it’s only for a few minutes.
Repetition is key in learning coding, so make sure to practice consistently.
Reading Coding Materials
Reading coding materials is an excellent way to learn more about coding syntax and logic.
There are numerous online resources available, such as blogs, documentation, and forums, that can aid in deepening your knowledge on coding.
Be sure to read the documentation for any coding language you want to learn.
Take notes while reading coding materials. It will help you understand the syntax and logic better.
Read:
Can You Learn Coding without a Degree?
Seeking Help From Experts
If you face challenging coding issues that you can’t seem to solve, it may be time to seek help from an expert.
Find a mentor or join a coding community to find experienced individuals who may assist you.
Google, Stack Overflow,
LearnCode
, and Reddit have vast communities of experts and beginners that can help you solve your coding problems.
Ask questions and engage in discussions with people who have more experience with coding as it can help you understand the syntax and logic more clearly.
Breakdown Complex Problems
Coding requires you to know how to break down complicated problems into smaller, manageable steps.
Start by identifying the problem’s various elements and then breaking it down into smaller sections.
Next, determine how to tackle each section before combining all the steps into a complete solution.
Breaking down complex problems will help you solve coding problems more effectively.
These four tips can assist you in understanding coding syntax and logic, whether you are a beginner or someone with experience. By practicing frequently, reading coding materials, seeking help from experts, and breaking down complex problems into manageable pieces, you can become a more competent coder.

By practicing frequently, reading coding materials, seeking help from experts, and breaking down complex problems into manageable pieces, you can become a more competent coder. Remember, it takes time, effort, and patience but with these tips, the process of learning can be made more accessible and enjoyable.
Read:
What is the Best Age to Start Learning Coding?
Conclusion
Understanding coding syntax and logic is essential for anyone who wants to become a proficient programmer. It allows you to write clean, efficient code that can be easily understood and maintained by others.
Consistent practice is vital to hone your coding skills and improve your understanding of syntax and logic. Regularly challenging yourself to solve programming problems can help you identify areas of weakness and improve your overall proficiency in coding.
As you continue to practice coding, it’s important to remember that mastery doesn’t happen overnight. It requires patience, persistence, and a willingness to learn from your mistakes. But with consistent effort and a growth mindset, anyone can become a great programmer.
So, keep coding, keep learning, and never give up on your dream of becoming a skilled programmer!

6 Tips to Improve Your Coding Speed [LAZY EDITION]
“I choose a lazy person to do a hard job. Because a lazy person will find an easy way to do it.” - Bill Gates
I couldn't agree more with this old quote from Bill Gates. I found that as I'm thriving as a developer, I tend to get lazier and the lazier I get, the more I acquire fast coding techniques. Let me share some of my favorite techniques with you folks.
1. Move cursor by word
Let's start with the most basic one. I believe most of us are already using this. It's simple, you can use the keyboard shortcuts Ctrl +←/→ (Windows) or Alt +←/→ (Mac)
Move cursor by word
Now you can navigate the codebase faster.
If you hold the Shift button along the way then it selects the word for you. Neat!
Highlight by word
2. Add cursors to line ends
Let's say you have to edit multiple lines of content to be the same, you can simply highlight all the lines and split the cursors to each line's end. Then you can edit those lines simultaneously.
Add cursors to line ends
The actual command name depends on the editor you use. Mine is
Visual Studio Code
and the exact command for this is "Add cursors to line ends". The shortcut key is Alt + Shift + I (Windows) or Command + Shift + l (Mac).
3.

Mine is
Visual Studio Code
and the exact command for this is "Add cursors to line ends". The shortcut key is Alt + Shift + I (Windows) or Command + Shift + l (Mac).
3. Add selection to next find match
When you want to replace some "A" with "B" but the "Replace All" function just replaces every occurrence and ruins the mood, try this.
Add selection to next find match
For
Visual Studio Code
, the shortcut is Ctrl + D (Windows) or Command + D (Mac).
4. Join lines
Sometimes after refactoring, you find out that a part of your code containing multiple lines can be reduced to a single line for better readability. Select all the lines and join them. For VS Code, that's the Join Lines command Ctrl + J (Windows) or Command + J (Mac).
Join lines
You might also notice that I've used tip number 3 to format the space between each function chain. However, if you have some code formatting or linting tool set up with an auto-fix feature, that will solve the problem right away.
Another example would be copying a multiline paragraph and storing it as a string variable.
Join a paragraph to a single line string
5. Move Line(s) Up/Down
Want to rearrange function order while refactoring or want to change the order of code execution? Have you been using the Cut/Paste method? Worry not, we have an easier way to do this.
Move multiple lines
Move a single line
Notice that the editor also takes into account the indentation style if the language syntax is recognized. For VS Code, the key command is Alt + Up/Down Arrow.
6. Duplicate selection
You can actually duplicate your current selection which is very useful for populating a list or any kind of collection depending on the context.
Duplicate selection
Extra: Use extensions
As mainstream editors are open to extension contributions, we can find many useful plugins that speed up our workflow. What extension to use depends on your use case. I can give you an example. Very often I have to create mock data with running numbers, so I ended up using
Insert Numbers
.
Insert numbers
As you can see, being lazy led me to find better and faster ways of finishing redundant steps.

Very often I have to create mock data with running numbers, so I ended up using
Insert Numbers
.
Insert numbers
As you can see, being lazy led me to find better and faster ways of finishing redundant steps. This might not feel like it has a big impact,  but when used frequently, it can be a huge time saver resulting in higher productivity.
7. Use multi-caret selection to edit multiple lines at once
Ever wished you could edit multiple lines of code at the same time? Well, you can! Multi-caret selection is a game-changer for making the same edit in multiple places at once. To use this feature, press Ctrl + Alt + mouse click (Windows) or Alt + Shift + mouse click (Mac) to create multiple carets. Then, you can use Ctrl + Shift + arrow keys to expand your selections.
For instance, if you want to add a semicolon at the end of several lines, simply create multiple carets at the end of each line and type a semicolon. Voilà! The semicolon is added to all the selected lines simultaneously. This feature is particularly handy when you want to make repetitive changes without the hassle of editing each line individually.
8. Use the “find and replace” feature to quickly update code
The “find and replace” feature is your best friend when it comes to quickly updating your code. You can use this tool to find specific words, phrases, or patterns and replace them with new ones. To access the “find and replace” feature, press Ctrl + F (Windows) or Command + F (Mac) for finding, and Ctrl + H (Windows) or Command + H (Mac) for replacing.
For example, if you want to replace all instances of a variable name with a new name, simply enter the old variable name in the “find” field and the new variable name in the “replace” field. Then, click the “replace all” button to update your code in one go. This feature saves you from the tedious task of manually searching and replacing each instance, making your coding process much more efficient.
9. Customize user and workspace settings to suit your needs
Customizing your user and workspace settings can significantly enhance your coding efficiency.

9. Customize user and workspace settings to suit your needs
Customizing your user and workspace settings can significantly enhance your coding efficiency. You can adjust settings like font size, color scheme, and keyboard shortcuts to match your preferences. To access these settings, go to the “settings” menu in your editor and select the options you want to customize.
For example, if you want to change the font size of your code, navigate to the “settings” menu and select the “font size” option. Choose the font size that suits you best. You can also customize keyboard shortcuts for specific actions, such as adding a new line or deleting a selection. By tailoring your user and workspace settings, you create a coding environment that is perfectly suited to your needs, helping you work more efficiently and comfortably.
Which one do you use most often?
I'd like to know which of these matches your preference. Also, I'd like to learn some cool techniques from you as well. Feel free to leave comments in the comment section to share your ideas and methods. If you find this useful please make sure to leave a like and share this blog post. Thank you!

My favorite tool is Ditto.  Multi clipboard.  Map it to control-shift-v and you get a list of your last (x) entries.  Arrow keys and you select what you want, hit enter, and it pastes.  I honestly believe that it has saved me years in programming time.  I'm old, so...
control-end, control-home, control-shift-end, control-shift-home, etc.  Just so dang useful.
